title: QUIC Server-Role Managed TLS Crypto Floor Architecture
purpose: Describe the permanent server-role managed TLS 1.3 crypto floor that lives behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam, accepts a supported peer ClientHello, constructs one local ServerHello, derives server-role Handshake packet-protection material from the resulting transcript boundary, and exposes outbound ServerHello bytes through the existing library-owned handshake seam without claiming wider handshake support.
design_summary: The connection library keeps server-role transcript parsing, local ServerHello construction, and Handshake key derivation as one narrow bridge-owned slice rather than adding a second TLS stack. The existing transcript-progress owner remains responsible for ordered Handshake CRYPTO assembly, deterministic message framing checks, and peer transport-parameter staging from ClientHello. Once that owner has accepted a supported ClientHello for TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1, the managed key-schedule owner consumes the peer key share plus the existing local ephemeral key pair, constructs one local ServerHello for the same subset, appends that ServerHello behind the accepted ClientHello at the transcript boundary, derives the client and server Handshake traffic secrets for QUIC packet protection, publishes explicit Handshake open/protect material through the bridge state, and emits the local ServerHello bytes as Handshake-level CryptoDataAvailable so the existing library seam can packetize them later. The slice deliberately stops after ServerHello and Handshake key availability: it does not synthesize EncryptedExtensions, certificate messages, server Finished, commit, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.
risks: The server-role crypto floor must stay behind the existing bridge seam and must not grow into a broad TLS subsystem.
The supported subset must stay pinned to TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1 until a later requirement explicitly widens it.
Outbound ServerHello availability must not be mistaken for EncryptedExtensions availability, certificate-flight ownership, handshake completion, or interop support.
Server-role commit must remain blocked even though Handshake keys become available at the ClientHello plus ServerHello transcript boundary.
